facts: Hybar LLC announced today that it raised $1.1 billion to expand its technologically advanced, environmentally focused operations in Osceola, Arkansas.
South Korean automotive supplier Kamtec Auto USA announced this week it will open its first U.S. facility in Montgomery, Alabama.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that ArcelorMittal Building Solutions will establish its North American operations in Atlanta.
Clarios, an American manufacturer of advanced, low-voltage battery technologies, announced today that it will expand its operations in St. Joseph.
Form Energy is transforming a former steel town into an advanced manufacturing hub for energy storage and battery technology.
Georgia Governor Brian P. Kemp announced that Dongwon Autopart Technology plans to invest $30 million in a new automotive parts facility in Georgia.
Texas Governor Greg Abbott delivered remarks and cut the ribbon at the Element3 Resources Permian Basin Lithium project in Midland, Texas.
Germany’s KettenWulf plans to invest $34 million in an advanced manufacturing operation in Auburn, Alabama.
River Parishes Community College (RPCC), in partnership with Hyundai Steel and state and local partners, launched workforce training programs in Donaldsonville, Louisiana.
The South Carolina Department of Commerce’s SC Nexus for Advanced Resilient Energy (SC NEXUS) division is partnering on advanced resilient energy supply chain initiatives.
source_title: SEDC News - Southern Economic Development Council
